title: bursera subtrifoliata
intro: Bursera subtrifoliata, also known as the Mexican copal or three-leaved copal tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Burseraceae. This tree has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which are derived from its resin. The health benefits of Bursera subtrifoliata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its resin can also be burned as an incense to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
